evals.prompt: I just redesigned the product landing page with a new hero section, feature cards, and dark mode toggle. How do I systematically inspect it visually before marking the redesign done?
evals.expected_output: User runs through all 7 categories: Layout (overflow at 375/768/1440px), Typography (readable at each breakpoint), Interactive (buttons hover states, dark mode toggle works), Hierarchy (hero is most prominent), Visual Separation (cards are distinct), Dark/Light Mode (both render, no invisible text), Edge Cases (long text wraps, empty states if applicable). Captures screenshots at minimum 3 breakpoints for each mode. Produces report in e2e-evidence/visual-inspection/report.md with findings by severity.

evals.prompt: We're testing iOS app redesign on simulator. The safe area looks correct on iPhone 12, but I need to verify it works across notched and non-notched devices. Walk me through.
evals.expected_output: User takes screenshots on multiple sim configs (iPhone 14 Pro Max with notch, iPhone SE no notch). Uses idb ui describe-all to check accessibility tree for safe area usage. Checklist item 5.1 (safe area respected). Reports if content is hidden behind notch or home indicator. Severity CRITICAL if hidden, HIGH if hard to use.
